typecodes.h

00001 /*
00002  *

00009  *

00013  * General Public License version 2 for more details (a copy is
00014  * included at /legal/license.txt). 
00015  * 
00016  * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License
00017  * version 2 along with this work; if not, write to the Free Software
00018  * Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin St, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA
00019  * 02110-1301 USA 
00020  * 
00021  * Please contact Sun Microsystems, Inc., 4150 Network Circle, Santa
00022  * Clara, CA 95054 or visit www.sun.com if you need additional
00023  * information or have any questions. 
00024  */
00025 
00026 /*
00027  * Type codes  6/12/91
00028 
00029     This typecode system allows us to represent the type
00030     of scalars in a uniform way. For instance, all integer types
00031     have some bits in common, and are distinguished by a built-in
00032     size field. Types without multiple sizes don't have a size field.
00033 
00034     Scalars may only have sizes which are powers of 2. The size
00035     field holds the log-based-2 of the object's size.
00036 
00037     All run-time types can be encoded in 4 bits. There are more
00038     compile- time types. These fit in 5 bits.
00039     Schematically, we have:
00040         +----+----+----+----+----+
00041         | c  |    t    |    s    |
00042         +----+----+----+----+----+
00043 
00044         Encoding is:
00045            c   t  s    type
00046         -------------------------
00047            0  00 00    unassigned
00048            0  00 01    array
00049            0  00 10    class
00050            0  00 11    proxy    (OBSOLETE)
00051            0  01 00    boolean (1 byte)
00052            0  01 01    char    (2 bytes)
00053            0  01 1s    float (single=1; double=2)
00054            0  1u xx    integer (byte=0; short=1; int=2; long=3;
00055                      u=1 => unsigned)
00056 
00057             For runtime types, the size of an object
00058             is 1<<(t&3)
00059 
00060            1  00 00    typedef    (compiler only)
00061            1  00 01    void    (compiler only)
00062            1  00 10    func    (compiler only)
00063            1  00 11    unknown (compiler only)
00064            1  01 00     error   (compiler only)
00065 
00066     Char and Boolean are not int's because they need a different signature,
00067     so have to be distinguishable, even at runtime. We allow arrays
00068     of objects, arrays(?), booleans, char, integers, and floats.
00069     Note that the low-order two bits of all these gives the log of
00070     the size, except for arrays, of course.
00071 
00072     I would prefer not to have unsigned int in the language, but
00073     don't want to make that decision at this level. We could come up
00074     with a better encoding of boolean and char if there were no
00075     unsigned.
00076 
00077     The compile-only type values that could be confused with the 
00078     integer and float scalar types must not ever be used. Value 0 must
00079     not be assigned a runtime type, as this is used for some non-trivial
00080     solution in punning types and pointer. In fact, we even have a name
00081     for it.
00082 */
00083 
00084 /* If you change these typecodes, you'll have to fix the arrayinfo table
00085    in gc.c and the {in,}direct_{load,store}_ops tables in
00086    compiler/tree2code.c */
00087 
00088 #ifndef _TYPECODES_H_
00089 #define _TYPECODES_H_
00090 
00091 #define T_NORMAL_OBJECT    0
00092 #define T_XXUNUSEDXX1   1    /* Used to be T_ARRAY */
00093 #define T_CLASS        2
00094 #define T_BOOLEAN    4
00095 #define T_CHAR        5
00096 
00097 #define T_FLOATING    4    /* add log2 size to get correct code:
00098                     float has code 6,
00099                     double has code 7 */
00100 #define T_INTEGER    010
00101 #define T_UINTEGER    014
00102 
00103 #define    T_MAXNUMERIC    020
00104 
00105 #define    T_XXUNUSEDXX2    020
00106 #define    T_VOID        021
00107 #define    T_FUNC        022
00108 #define    T_UNKNOWN    023
00109 #define    T_ERROR        024
00110 
00111 /* for type construction */
00112 #define T_TMASK    034
00113 #define T_LMASK 003
00114 #define T_LSIZE 2
00115 #define T_MKTYPE( t, l )  ( ( (t)&T_TMASK ) | ( (l)&T_LMASK) )
00116 
00117 /* for type deconstruction */
00118     /*
00119      * Because we promise always to let ints and compile-only types be 
00120      * distinguished by the "t" and "s" bits above, we can simplify
00121      * some of our predicates by masking out the "c" bit when testing
00122      * for integers. Thus the T_TS_MASK...
00123      */
00124 #define T_TS_MASK 034
00125 #define T_ISINTEGER(t)  ( ((t)&030) == T_INTEGER  )
00126 #define T_ISFLOATING(t) ( ((t)&036) == T_FLOAT )
00127 #define T_ISNUMERIC(t)  ( (t) >= T_CHAR && (t) < T_MAXNUMERIC )
00128 #define T_SIZEFIELD(t)    ((t)&T_LMASK)
00129 #define T_ELEMENT_SIZE(t) (1<<T_SIZEFIELD(t))    /* only for some!! */
00130 
00131 #define T_IS_BIG_TYPE(t) ((t == T_DOUBLE) || (t == T_LONG))
00132 #define T_TYPE_WORDS(t) (T_IS_BIG_TYPE(t) ? 2 : 1)
00133 
00134 /* nick-names for the usual scalar types */
00135 #define T_FLOAT  T_MKTYPE(T_FLOATING,2)
00136 #define T_DOUBLE T_MKTYPE(T_FLOATING,3)
00137 #define T_BYTE     T_MKTYPE(T_INTEGER,0)
00138 #define T_SHORT     T_MKTYPE(T_INTEGER,1)
00139 #define T_INT     T_MKTYPE(T_INTEGER,2)
00140 #define T_LONG     T_MKTYPE(T_INTEGER,3)
00141 
00142 #ifdef NO_LONGER_USED
00143 /* We no longer support these types */
00144 #define T_UBYTE     T_MKTYPE(T_UINTEGER,0)
00145 #define T_USHORT T_MKTYPE(T_UINTEGER,1)
00146 #define T_UINT     T_MKTYPE(T_UINTEGER,2)
00147 #define T_ULONG     T_MKTYPE(T_UINTEGER,3)
00148 
00149 #endif
00150 
00151 /* only a slight exaggeration */
00152 #define N_TYPECODES    (1<<6)
00153 #define    N_TYPEMASK    (N_TYPECODES-1)
00154 
00155 #endif /* !_TYPECODES_H_ */
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:28 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
